big_picture: India cosponsored a UNSC resolution condemning attacks by Iran against GCC nations and Jordan. The Supreme Court ruled that parental salary alone cannot determine OBC Creamy Layer Status. Bomb threats were reported to the Maharashtra legislature and four other locations. The Supreme Court dismissed a plea by Advocate Ashok Pandey seeking Rs 1 crore in fees from the Union Government for cases related to former Chief Justice Dipak Misra. In Parliament, Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge demanded that Prime Minister Narendra Modi address the economic impact of the conflict in West Asia.

articles.title: How the Iran war could trigger a fresh food crisis
articles.summary: The conflict in Iran is increasing energy and fertilizer costs, which could lead to higher food prices and lower agricultural yields. Farmers are concerned about resource shortages that may affect harvests. This situation could push India to take policy measures to stabilize food supply and prices.
articles.raw_summary: The Iran conflict is driving up energy and fertilizer costs, raising alarms over a return of food inflation. Farmers are bracing for resource shortages that could drag down harvests.

articles.title: Explained — Why oil prices are still rising despite IEA decision on emergency reserves
articles.summary: Oil prices continue to rise despite the International Energy Agency's decision to release emergency reserves. Analysts say this doesn't address other global economic problems, such as disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, where tanker traffic has nearly stopped. This could affect India's fuel imports and energy security, potentially prompting policy action.
articles.raw_summary: Analysts believe that the IEA decision does not do much to address the other problems that the global economy would face. Most of the refined products, including Jet fuel, still flow through the Strait of Hormuz, where tanker traffic has all but halted, as acknowledged by Birol as well.

The case highlights the role of regulatory bodies in enforcing tax policies.

articles.title: No DAPE Where Distributors Act on Principal-to-Principal Basis ITAT Delhi
articles.summary: The Tribunal ruled that a Dependent Agent PE arises only if agents habitually conclude contracts or secure orders on behalf of the foreign enterprise. articles.title: Parental Salary Alone Can't Decide OBC Creamy Layer Status : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that parental salary alone cannot determine OBC creamy layer status. The court emphasized that the status and category of the parent's job are also essential factors. This decision impacts reservation policies and eligibility for government benefits under the OBC quota.
articles.raw_summary: The status as well as the category of post to which a candidate's parent or parents belong is essential, the Court noted.

articles.title: Parallel GST Proceedings Barred as Section 6(2)(b) Prohibits Dual Adjudication
articles.summary: Indian courts have ruled that once a GST authority issues a show cause notice, no other authority can initiate separate proceedings on the same issue and tax period. This decision is based on Section 6(2)(b) of the GST law, which prohibits dual adjudication. The ruling ensures clarity and prevents overlapping proceedings, which could lead to confusion and legal disputes for taxpayers.
articles.raw_summary: Courts have clarified that once one GST authority initiates proceedings by issuing a show cause notice, the other authority cannot start separate proceedings on the same issue and tax period. articles.title: SC: OBC Creamy Layer Status Cannot Be Based Solely on Parental Income
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that determining OBC creamy layer status cannot rely solely on parental income, stating that treating children of PSU and private sector employees differently from government staff for reservation benefits is discriminatory. This decision affects reservation policies and could influence future government actions on affirmative action.

articles.title: Mahindra Susten Secures ₹10.25 Billion for 300 MW Renewable Project in Rajasthan
articles.summary: Mahindra Susten, a clean-tech arm of the Mahindra Group, has secured ₹10.25 billion in financing from HDFC Bank for a 300 MW renewable energy project in Rajasthan. The project, developed by its subsidiary Hazel Hybren, will supply power to NHPC and supports India's push for renewable energy. This investment highlights private sector participation in India's clean energy transition.

The project, developed by Hazel Hybren, a wholly owned subsidiary of Mahindra Susten, secured ₹10.25 billion (~$111.32 million) in financing from HDFC Bank. articles.title: Industry Groups Slam Karnataka C&I Power Tariff Hike to Fund Farm Subsidy
articles.summary: The Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission (KERC) has increased electricity tariffs for commercial and industrial consumers to help fund free power subsidies for farmers. Industry groups have criticized the move, arguing it will raise costs for businesses. This decision highlights the trade-off between industrial growth and agricultural subsidies in Karnataka.
articles.raw_summary: Trade bodies have sharply criticized the Karnataka Electricity Regulatory Commission’s (KERC) decision to revise electricity tariffs for FY 2025-26, raising charges for commercial and industrial (C I) consumers to help bridge a subsidy gap arising from free power for farmers.

articles.summary: The Punjab Congress has filed a complaint with the National Commission for Women (NCW) against Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann, alleging he made derogatory remarks about women during an International Women's Day event. The opposition party seeks action from the NCW, raising concerns about gender sensitivity in public discourse. This incident could influence future policies on women's rights and political accountability.

articles.title: Middle East crisis: India backs UNSC resolution condemning Iranian strikes on Gulf nations
articles.summary: India backed a UN Security Council that strongly condemned Iran's attacks on regional neighbours, including Saudi Arabia and the UAE, with 13 votes in favour. The resolution demands an immediate halt to strikes on civilian areas and interference with maritime trade. China and Russia abstained, deeming the text biased, while Iran labelled it unjust.

The resolution demands an immediate halt to strikes on civilian areas and interference with maritime trade. China and Russia abstained, deeming the text biased, while Iran labelled it unjust.

articles.title: Railway Travel Insurance Cannot Be Limited To Online Tickets, Must Be Available To Counter Ticket Passengers Too: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that passengers buying railway tickets at counters must also have access to travel insurance, just like those booking online. Currently, only online ticket buyers can opt for insurance at a small extra cost. The court's order ensures equal access to this benefit for all passengers.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has observed that passengers purchasing railway tickets over the counter cannot be denied the benefit of travel insurance when the same facility is available to those booking tickets online. A bench of Justices Ahsanuddin Amanullah and R. Mahadevan �noted that passengers booking railway tickets online are able to opt for insurance at a nominal additional cost, whereas the same option is not presently available to passengers purchasing tickets physically at railway counters. articles.title: Supreme Court directs CBI to conduct preliminary inquiry into deaths of 3 Dalits in MP
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) to conduct a preliminary inquiry into the deaths of three Dalits in Madhya Pradesh. This order comes amid concerns over caste-based violence and the need for accountability. The case highlights systemic issues related to civil liberties and the protection of marginalized communities in India.

articles.title: Adani Total Gas shares soar nearly 40% in just 2 days! What’s triggering the rally despite weak market?
articles.summary: The Indian government issued the Natural Gas (Supply Regulation) Order, 2026, to prioritize gas supplies to essential sectors amid disruptions caused by the Middle East conflict. This move follows LNG shipment issues through the Strait of Hormuz and aims to stabilize energy markets. Adani Total Gas shares surged nearly 40% in two days due to the policy announcement.
articles.raw_summary: Shares of Adani Total Gas rose sharply on Thursday, extending a two-day rally after the government issued the Natural Gas (Supply Regulation) Order, 2026, to prioritise gas supplies to essential sectors amid Middle East conflict-driven disruptions. The move follows LNG shipment issues through the Strait of Hormuz, while supply concerns continue to roil energy markets.

articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will release the 22nd installment of the PM-Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme, benefiting 9.32 crore farmers with ₹18,640 crore in direct transfers. This scheme provides income support to small and marginal farmers, ensuring financial stability and agricultural growth. The announcement highlights the government's commitment to farmer welfare and rural development.

articles.title: अंतर्राष्ट्रीय योग दिवस 2026 के 100 दिन शेष रहने के उपलक्ष्य में योग महोत्सव का आयोजन
articles.summary: The government is organizing a Yoga Mahotsav (festival) to mark 100 days remaining until International Yoga Day 2026. The event aligns with India's policy focus on promoting yoga and wellness as part of cultural and public health initiatives. While it highlights government engagement, it does not indicate new policy actions or regulatory changes.

articles.title: पर्यावरण, वन और जलवायु परिवर्तन मंत्रालय ने हाथी और रेल के बीच होने वाले हादसों की चुनौती से निपटने के लिए 2 दिवसीय कार्यशाला का आयोजन किया
articles.summary: The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change held a two-day workshop to tackle the issue of elephant-rail accidents. The event involved stakeholders to discuss solutions for reducing such incidents, which have implications for wildlife conservation and railway safety in India.

articles.title: पीएम राहत- सड़क दुर्घटना पीड़ितों का कैशलेस इलाज
articles.summary: The Indian government has launched the PM Rahaat scheme to provide cashless medical treatment for road accident victims. This initiative aims to ensure immediate healthcare access without financial barriers. The scheme is part of broader efforts to improve road safety and emergency medical services in India.

articles.summary: Union Home and Cooperation Minister Amit Shah responded to a no-confidence motion against the Lok Sabha Speaker in Parliament. The debate highlights political tensions and could impact governance dynamics. The outcome may influence parliamentary procedures and opposition-government relations.

articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated and laid the foundation for several development projects in Ernakulam, Kerala. These projects are part of government initiatives aimed at boosting infrastructure and economic growth in the region. The event highlights the central government's focus on regional development through policy-driven investments.

This could impact how social media platforms operate in India and how government information is disseminated. The move suggests a formal step in regulating digital communication.

articles.summary: The Indian government has supported the International Energy Agency's (IEA) decision to release emergency oil reserves due to current supply disruptions. This move is aimed at stabilizing oil markets and ensuring energy security. The government's endorsement highlights its alignment with global efforts to manage energy crises.

articles.summary: Kerala's railway budget for 2026-27 has crossed ₹3,795 crore, marking a tenfold increase compared to the annual expenditure of ₹372 crore during 2009-14. This reflects the state government's focus on infrastructure development and highlights a major policy decision in railway funding.

articles.title: राष्ट्रपति आज विज्ञान भवन, नई दिल्ली में हुए जल महोत्सव 2026 के राष्ट्रीय कार्यक्रम में शामिल हुईं

The statement highlights India's foreign policy priorities and diplomatic engagement in the region. This reflects the government's stance on regional cooperation and stability.

The visit likely pertains to government efforts in skill development or entrepreneurship, though no specific policy announcements were made. The IIE is a key institution under MSDE, focusing on training and capacity building.

articles.summary: Union Minister Dr. Jitendra Singh met with a delegation of Ethiopian women parliamentarians and shared India's globally recognized governance practices. The discussion highlights India's efforts to promote its policy frameworks internationally, which could influence future bilateral or multilateral collaborations.

articles.title: TNERC Adopts Tariff for Two 15 MW Solar Plus 45 MWh Battery Storage Projects
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Electricity Regulatory Commission (TNERC) has approved a tariff of ₹4.47 per kWh for two 15 MW solar projects with 45 MWh battery storage in Karur and Thiruvarur districts. The projects will be implemented under a 25-year power purchase agreement (PPA), with Evolve Green Energies selected for the Karur project. This decision supports Tamil Nadu's renewable energy goals and sets a precedent for similar projects in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tamil Nadu Electricity Regulatory Commission (TNERC) has approved a tariff of ₹4.47 (~$0.048)/kWh for two 15 MW solar projects integrated with 45 MWh battery energy storage systems in the Karur and Thiruvarur districts. The projects will be implemented under a 25-year power purchase agreement (PPA). For the Karur project, the Commission allowed Tamil Nadu Green Energy Corporation (TNGECL) to issue a letter of award to Evolve Green Energies, the L1 bidder.

articles.title: Iran's Hormuz control gives country leverage despite military struggles

articles.title: West Asia conflict could hurt agri input availability: UPL Executive
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are increasing costs for agricultural inputs like fertilizers, with prices rising 50-80%. While current inventories ensure stable supply for the upcoming kharif season, sustained disruptions could force policy interventions to stabilize prices and ensure food security in India.

While shipping, insurance, and energy costs have climbed, immediate agrochemical availability for the upcoming kharif season remains stable due to pre-built inventories.

articles.title: Iran war impact: Govt mulling diversion of stranded cargo to new markets
articles.summary: India is assessing the diversion of export cargoes stranded by the West Asia crisis to alternative markets. articles.title: Centre holds back on fuel exports ban due to logistics, tax hurdles as West Asia conflict rages
articles.summary: India is not banning fuel exports despite global supply issues. Logistical hurdles, contractual obligations with overseas buyers, and tax waivers for export-only refineries are key reasons. Reliance Industries and Nayara Energy are major exporters. China and Thailand have curbed their exports. India aims to avoid adding to global market turbulence. Domestic refiners prefer selling to state retailers for better returns.

Nonetheless, this surge of positivity is the strongest we've witnessed in ten years.

This aims to boost domestic production and attract foreign capital. The government is streamlining processes for greater certainty for investors.

Higher fuel costs may affect consumers, while oil companies might absorb initial burdens. This scenario could limit interest rate cuts.
